interactables.name: War Table
interactables.text: Maps of Thornwood with burn routes marked in red ink. Supply lines, patrol schedules, alchemical drop points. Across the center of Ashvale's map, written in bold strokes: 'Salt the Root — Phase 3.' The Church doesn't want to save this village. They want to destroy what's beneath it.

interactables.name: Nightcap Root Barrel
interactables.text: Barrels stamped with the Church of Ashen Light's seal, filled with dried nightcap root — enough to poison a dozen wells for years. Definitive proof. The Church is the poisoner. The blight, the dying crops, the sick children — all orchestrated.

interactables.name: Iron Cage
interactables.text: Small. Child-sized. Empty now. But the scratches on the bars tell a story of desperate fingers and broken nails. A scrap of fabric clings to the lock — a child's nightshirt. This connects to Fenrick's smuggling. The children didn't escape. They were taken.
interactables.state_alt_text: Fenrick's 'rescued' children were brought here. The Church used them as leverage — or worse, as test subjects for the nightcap root dosages. The fabric matches the orphanage blankets.

interactables.name: Alchemist's Journal
interactables.text:
A leather-bound journal in precise handwriting:
'Day 14: Nightcap root concentration increased to 3 drams per barrel. Village well contamination proceeding as planned.
Day 28: Crops showing expected blight pattern. The healer suspects water but lacks proof.
Day 41: Phase 2 complete. Village weakened. Phase 3 — Salt the Root — awaits the Archdeacon's order.
Day 55: Brother Maren suspects nothing. The fool prays while we poison his flock.'
The formula is detailed in full. Undeniable evidence of the Church's conspiracy.

interactables.name: Ash Sigil Circle
interactables.text: A ritual circle drawn in ash on the stone floor. But these aren't Ashen Light prayers — the symbols are older. Druidic. Inverted. Someone in the Church understands the old magic well enough to pervert it. They're not just poisoning the water. They're trying to sever the covenant from their end.
interactables.state_alt_text: The inverted druidic symbols mirror the ones in the Thornwood circle — but twisted, broken. Where the druids wrote 'give,' the Church wrote 'take.' Where the covenant says 'freely,' the Church says 'by force.' This isn't faith. It's magical warfare.
